body.text: Texas felt different the moment I crossed the state line.
The energy shifted and I knew I made the right decision. For me. For my kid.
I got into fitness and started living at the gym. I got in the best shape of my life   — and met lots of new friends that fueled the energy.
Those friends helped me clear the wreckage and start to rebuild.
I immersed myself into personal development — 80+ books in my first year alone.
I started attending personal development conferences and seminars — and met even more friends who were building, growing, and reaching.
As my circle grew, so did the opportunities. High performance coaching. Business consulting. Fun and friends around every corner.
Life was good. I was able to be the mom I wanted to be — and that was always the goal.
I still struggled. The turbulence under the surface — depression, anxiety, trauma — still played a prominent role.
It wasn't a straight ascent. But the altitude was increasing.
Thing was — all this opportunity around me. All this growth. I still didn't feel like I knew who I was or where I belonged.
